compositional_truth_check.notes: The biome is experiencing a catastrophic loss of biological material. The registry (P1-P4) is largely unsupported by current visual data.

inventory_reconciliation.P3_Pothos: Systemic Loss (White rabbit anchor present, but no plant matter)

plant_audit.P3: White rabbit anchor present; soil is bare. No Pothos detected.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Dark, potentially damp, but devoid of any vegetative growth.
biome_observations.debris: Eggshell fragments observed in the foreground pot; these appear to be an intentional intervention or mulch attempt.
biome_observations.surface: Desk surface is clear of organic matter.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-4: Initial observation shows a single green sprout in the foreground pot.
temporal_deltas.t-4_to_t-3: Sprout remains visible; no significant change.
temporal_deltas.t-3_to_t-2: Sprout appears to be wilting or losing turgor pressure.
temporal_deltas.t-2_to_t-1: Sprout has collapsed or been removed; total loss of visible plant matter.

visual_health_inference.conclusion: The biome is currently non-viable. All registered specimens are absent or deceased.
visual_health_inference.evidence: Total absence of chlorophyll-bearing tissue across all monitored pots.

anomalies: Presence of eggshell fragments in the foreground pot (New Intervention).
Complete disappearance of the sprout observed in early images.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a rapid decline of the botanical environment. Starting from a state where at least one sprout was present, the system has regressed to a state of total biological vacancy. The presence of eggshell fragments suggests an attempt at soil enrichment or physical marking, but this has not prevented the systemic loss of all registered plants. The environment is currently devoid of life.
